nodes.label: 'Why do some groups experience greater mobility than others?' — Quarto revealjs presentation (primary source document) proposing a unified framework linking absolute and relative intergenerational mobility, with a parameter-swap counterfactual decomposition method
nodes.author: mauricio bucca

nodes.label: Heteroskedastic IGE (intergenerational elasticity) workhorse model: y|x ~ Normal(alpha + beta*x, sigma(x)), with beta as the persistence parameter (~0.2-0.6 empirically) — foundation for linking relative and absolute mobility
nodes.author: mauricio bucca

nodes.label: Unified analytical framework treating upward/downward/immobile absolute-mobility outcomes as probabilistic results of four mechanisms (income floor alpha, persistence beta, uncertainty sigma, parental income x) derived from the IGE model via standardized thresholds and the normal CDF
nodes.author: mauricio bucca

nodes.label: Parameter-swap counterfactual decomposition method: isolates each structural parameter's (alpha, beta, sigma) marginal contribution to group differences in absolute mobility, plus aggregation (fixed distribution) and composition (distributional swap) effects
nodes.author: mauricio bucca

nodes.label: Empirical application to PSID 1968-2019 data: 12,445 Black and White sons/daughters born 1952-1989, Bayesian hierarchical estimation of group-specific alpha/beta/gamma/lambda parameters for labor income mobility
nodes.author: mauricio bucca

nodes.label: Key finding: Black men show much lower intergenerational persistence (beta≈0.19 vs 0.35 for white men) and greater income volatility — 'perverse openness' where upward mobility from low origins is possible but gains are modest, uncertain, and less transmissible; offsetting composition/slope effects yield near aggregate parity in absolute mobility between races
nodes.author: mauricio bucca

nodes.label: Blau & Duncan (1967) — cited as origin of the 'perverse openness' concept describing weak intergenerational transmission with modest/uncertain upward gains
